description:
Port of fz-aiz.3e from the unmerged `fz-aiz/any-key-floor` branch.
Retires the `.29.11` panic family (no covering spec for FnId on
Direct/TailCall) by making `SpecRegistry::resolve` total over
reachable FnIds via subsumption.

## Verified state on main

- `ModuleTypes.any_key_specs: HashMap<FnId, Vec<Descr>>` exists at
  ir_typer.rs:155. O(1) index, but NOT total — only populated when
  the typer's reachability walk genuinely needed an any-key
  (closure-reachable, entry-seeded). Direct-call-only fns have no
  any-key today.
- `ModuleTypes.naturally_opaque` does NOT exist.
- `.29.11` panic at ir_codegen.rs:4033 fires when resolve can't find
  a covering spec.

## What lands

### (a) `ensure_any_keys(specs, m)` in ir_typer.rs

After the reachability fixpoint converges, walk every FnId that has
any spec; if `(fid, vec![any; arity])` isn't already in `specs`,
synthesize one via `type_fn(f, m, Some(&any_key))` and insert.

Result: every reachable FnId has at least the any-key, so
`SpecRegistry::resolve` returns Some for any query via subsumption
(`q ⊆ any` for all `q`). The `.29.11` panic stops being reachable
for any reachable fn.

### (b) `ModuleTypes.naturally_opaque: HashSet<FnId>`

Snapshotted from `specs.keys()` BEFORE `ensure_any_keys` augments
the map. Identifies fns whose any-key was *naturally* seeded by the
reachability pass — i.e., fns reachable via opaque dispatch.

Splits the conflated old meaning of `any_key_specs.contains(fid)`:
- `any_key_specs` → existence signal (post-augmentation: total)
- `naturally_opaque` → routing signal (selective)

### (c) Closure body picker uses `naturally_opaque`

Two sites today depend on `any_key_specs` for routing:
- `lower_prim::Prim::MakeClosure` (ir_codegen.rs:6307) — builds key
  from captures + any-fill, resolves, falls back to first
  registered SpecId at 6337–6340.
- `closure_shapes` builder (~ir_codegen.rs:2025).

After (a), every reachable FnId has an any-key, so the existing
"first registered" fallback would route direct-only fns to the
any-key body — wrong, because their closure's stored body_func_id
should point at a narrow spec. Decision tree post-split:
- opaque-reachable fn (in `naturally_opaque`) → respect resolve
  (any-key is the right answer for opaque dispatch).
- direct-only fn (not in `naturally_opaque`) → substitute first
  narrow when resolve returns the any-key (which only happens for
  zero-capture / all-any queries).

### (d) Audit other readers of `any_key_specs`

Under the new model, `any_key_specs` is total over reachable FnIds,
so any caller that meant 'does this fn participate in opaque
dispatch' should switch to `naturally_opaque`. Known readers:
- ir_typer.rs:184 (`ModuleTypes::any_key_spec`)
- ir_typer.rs:194 (`ModuleTypes::any_spec_for`)
- ir_inline.rs:727 (any-key removal during inlining)

Each must be inspected for intent. Cite findings in PR description.

## Acceptance

- `.29.11` panic no longer reachable in fixture matrix.
- Closure routing parity confirmed against apply2, higher_order,
  curried_add, closure_typed_captures, actor_ring,
  three_process_chain.
- Per-FnId reader audit documented in PR.
- 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests green.
- Goldens reblessed: `expected.specs` grows by one any-key entry
  per reachable FnId (offset later by fz-aiz.7-style seed drop —
  out of scope here).

comments.text: Closed in favor of fz-rcp.5's architecture. The any-key floor was scaffolding to make resolve total; the right fix is to stop having codegen call resolve at all (read the typer's published outcome). See fz-rcp epic comment for the reasoning. The naturally_opaque routing field, the closure-body-picker coupling, and the cont-ABI hazards all dissolve when codegen reads outcomes directly.

title: fz-rcp.3: ensure_any_keys soundness floor + naturally_opaque split
